title: Healthcare Workflow Agent and Tool Hooks

hook_methods.trigger: Called before each agent turn (each time current agent changes)

hook_methods.method: on_agent_end(context: RunContextWrapper[HealthcareSupportContext], agent: Agent, output: Any) -> None
hook_methods.trigger: Called when agent produces a final output

hook_methods.method: on_tool_start(context: RunContextWrapper[HealthcareSupportContext], agent: Agent, tool: Tool) -> None
hook_methods.trigger: Called immediately before a local tool is invoked
hook_methods.action: cast context to ToolContext; emit('tool_start', agent, tool.name, call_id, arguments)

hook_methods.method: on_tool_end(context: RunContextWrapper[HealthcareSupportContext], agent: Agent, tool: Tool, result: str) -> None
hook_methods.trigger: Called immediately after a local tool returns
hook_methods.action: cast context to ToolContext; emit('tool_end', agent, tool.name, call_id, output=_to_jsonable(result))

base_hooks_not_overridden: on_llm_start(context, agent, system_prompt, input_items) — no-op in WorkflowHooks
on_llm_end(context, agent, response) — no-op in WorkflowHooks
on_handoff(context, from_agent, to_agent) — no-op in WorkflowHooks
activity_flow: AGENT LOOP — Runner.run(orchestrator, prompt, context, session, hooks=hooks):
→ Runner calls LLM for agent turn
→ [HOOK] WorkflowHooks.on_tool_start → emit('tool_start', agent, tool, call_id, args)
→ [HOOK] WorkflowHooks.on_tool_end  → emit('tool_end', agent, tool, call_id, output)
[handoff] → update current agent, re-run loop (on_agent_start fires again)
→ Runner.run(orchestrator, state, session, hooks=hooks)  [hooks fire again per above]
→ _copy_output_files() → copy sandbox output artifacts + fallback docs
→ [HOOK] on_agent_start / on_tool_start / on_tool_end / on_agent_end fire as above

helper_function.name: _to_jsonable(value: Any) -> Any
helper_function.purpose: Serialize hook output to JSON-safe value; handles Pydantic BaseModel, primitives, dicts/lists, and fallback str()

hooks_attachment_points: Runner.run(orchestrator, state, ..., hooks=hooks)   — resume after interruption
policy_agent.as_tool(..., hooks=hooks)              — sandbox policy sub-agent as tool
key_relationships: WorkflowHooks depends_on ToolContext (cast for tool_call_id, tool_arguments)
run_healthcare_support_workflow creates and owns WorkflowHooks instance
Runner consumes WorkflowHooks via hooks= parameter
WorkflowHooks is shared across orchestrator, memory_recap, and sandbox_policy_tool runs
important_notes: WorkflowHooks overrides only 4 of 7 RunHooksBase methods (no on_llm_start, on_llm_end, on_handoff). All hooks are async. Each hook emits a named event via HealthcareSupportContext.emit(), enabling UI/frontend observability. on_tool_start/end cast context to ToolContext to access tool_call_id and tool_arguments. Interruption loop capped at 5 approval rounds.
